title: CXXRTL: Unflattened designs cause assertion failures
body:
In cxxrtl you can pass a flag `-noflatten` to prevent the `flatten` pass being run before elaboration during `write_cxxrtl`, however, a number of issues prevent non-flat modules from functioning.

During elaboration by cxxrtl there's a desire to flatten modules to allow inlining and unbuffering of nets, by doing so it can be possible to avoid delta iterations during simulation. 

While debugging #2516 I attempted to use `-noflatten` to attempt to avoid this behavior without affecting other debug settings, I hit the assertion here, due to my top level module having reset assigned to a constant `1'b0` value. Which fails the `is_wire()` check. https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/b72c29465392c8d260ddf55def169438f7fb64b2/backends/cxxrtl/cxxrtl_backend.cc#L1328

While attempting to workaround the issue by not using a constant driver, I hit the assert here https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/b72c29465392c8d260ddf55def169438f7fb64b2/backends/cxxrtl/cxxrtl_backend.cc#L1321

I'm fairly confident that this is a bug, however it could also be intended behavior that's not adequately documented at the moment. 

For a testcase you can take the case from #2516 and modify `cxxrtl.ys` to have `write_cxxrtl -noflatten` instead of `write_cxxrtl`, that should hit the case.
